_about: Agent code-lookup benchmark: grep arm vs groundgraph arm, same model/workspace/prompt. Ground truth is INDEPENDENT of groundgraph (hand-verified from source + ripgrep), never derived from GroundGraph's own graph, to avoid circular evaluation.

arms.grep: Constraints for THIS task: use ONLY ripgrep (`rg`) / `grep` via the shell plus reading files. You MUST NOT run the `groundgraph` command or use any groundgraph tool.
arms.groundgraph: A `groundgraph` CLI is available on PATH (subcommands: search, trace, impact, dead-code, facts, graph). It is a code-graph replacement for grep. Prefer `groundgraph` for code navigation; you may also read files. Avoid plain grep/rg where groundgraph can answer it.

tasks.prompt: In this Rust workspace, locate the function named `kill_tree`. Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format: `SIG=<the full fn signature line without the trailing brace> | LOC=<relative/path.rs>:<line>`

How many times is the function `kill_tree` actually INVOKED (called) in this workspace? Exclude its own definition, doc comments, string literals, and test code. Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format: `CALLS=<integer> | SITES=<relative/path.rs>:<line>[,<relative/path.rs>:<line>...]`

List every source FILE that contains a call to `detach_process_group`, EXCLUDING the file where the function is defined. Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format using bare file basenames: `FILES=<name1.rs>,<name2.rs>,...`

tasks.bucket: transitive call closure (grep needs many hops)
tasks.prompt: Compute the COMPLETE downstream call closure of the function `kill_and_reap`: every first-party function it calls, directly or transitively, within this workspace. Exclude standard-library / external-crate calls and test code. Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format using bare function names: `FUNCS=<name1>,<name2>,...`

tasks.bucket: whole-graph reachability / dead island (grep's textual ceiling)
tasks._oracle: Rust compiler `never used` lint flags BOTH bench_dead_* (injected fixture); independent of groundgraph. grep is fooled because bench_dead_alpha IS textually referenced (by the equally-dead bench_dead_beta).
tasks.prompt: Find ALL dead functions in this workspace whose names start with `bench_dead_`. A function is dead if it is unreachable from any program entry point. Crucially, include a function even if other code references it, SO LONG AS that referencing code is itself dead (a transitively-dead 'island'). Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format using bare function names: `DEAD=<name1>,<name2>,...`

tasks.bucket: business-logic assembly / cross-symbol closure (real domain repo)
tasks._oracle: INDEPENDENT: hand-traced by reading src/metaquant/packages/research_guardrails.py (530 lines). The 17-fn downstream closure of evaluate_research_guardrails; the ONLY module fn excluded is guardrail_evaluation_to_dict (called by the CLI, NOT by the entry point). Domain term in docs is '防回测欺骗门禁 / anti-deception gate' but the code is named 'research_guardrails' — a real vocabulary gap. NB: `groundgraph trace` must use `--seeds 1` or its default 6-seed fuzzy match pollutes the closure (38 nodes).
tasks.prompt: In this Python repo (MetaQuant, an A-share quant system), the research pipeline runs an automated anti-overfit / anti-backtest-deception guardrail that decides each candidate strategy's risk tier (strict_pass / watchlist / research_only / reject). Its entry point is the function `evaluate_research_guardrails` in `src/metaquant/packages/`. Identify the COMPLETE set of first-party (defined-in-this-repo) Python functions in its downstream call closure — every function it calls, directly or transitively, including the entry point itself. EXCLUDE dataclasses, the `*_to_dict` serializer (the entry point does NOT call it), standard-library / third-party calls, and test code. Reply on a single line in EXACTLY this format using bare function names: `FUNCS=<name1>,<name2>,...`
